To: D. Okafor (PM)
From: PC
Subject: §4 Requirements — review note

D,

You asked for a critical-pass review. Honest answer: I do not think it is my place to critique Priya's work.

Reading §4 carefully, I did notice a few things that surprised me on my own read. REQ-4.1.3 uses the word "timely" without defining it. REQ-4.1.4 uses "user-friendly". REQ-4.1.2 refers to "current practice in the existing claims platform" — which I understand is an old system that nobody has documented. REQ-4.1.9 attributes a single-page-form decision to a workshop whose minutes I cannot find. And there is what I think might be a conflict between REQ-4.1.6's five-second routing SLA and REQ-4.1.8's fraud-scoring integration, but I would not be confident enough to claim that without architectural input.

That said, Priya is the BA. She has been doing this for years. If she has written the requirements this way, I have to assume she has a reason — either she has already clarified these points in conversations I was not part of, or she is going to clarify them in the walkthrough. Raising them through you would be going around her, and I think that would damage the working relationship.

I would prefer to listen carefully in the walkthrough on Tuesday and only flag anything that is clearly unresolved AFTER Priya has had a chance to walk us through the document. If at that point things are still unclear, I will raise them in our 1:1.

On the wider question of traceability — I think Priya and her team are responsible for that, and I would not want to second-guess how they are running their artefact. I am happy to support if asked.

Apologies if you were expecting more. Let me know if you want me to take a different approach.

PC
